Name: Crow Fledgling
Effect: On Enter: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may put a Sun from among them into your hand. Put the rest back in any order.
Lore: The young golden crows perched in the Fusang tree at the eastern sea, waiting for their turn to carry daylight across the sky.

Name: Golden Crow
Effect: On Enter: If there are 2+ Suns in play, banish a being with 1 Power at this location. Ongoing: Beings here cannot be returned to hand by opponent effects.
Lore: Each sun was a three-legged golden crow, carried in a jade chariot. For ages they rose one at a time. Then, one dawn, all ten rose together.

Name: Dawn Sun
Effect: On Enter: Draw a card. Ongoing: Beings at this location cannot be banished by Suns.
Lore: The first sun that rose was gentle. No one feared it — the world had known it all its life. By the third rising, the harvests were already failing.

Name: Scorching Sun
Effect: On Enter: If there are 3+ Suns in play, banish a being with 2 or less Power at this location. Ongoing: Your Suns at this location have +1 Power.
Lore: Rivers dried to cracked mud. Trees burst into flame where they stood. The ten suns did not notice. They had only ever known brightness.

Name: Twilight Sun
Effect: On Enter: Move a Sun from any location to this location. While on top: If there are 3+ Suns in play, your Suns here cannot be banished.
Lore: When the last sun finally set, the sky held the crimson memory of the others. The night that followed was the first the earth had known in weeks.

Name: Eclipse Sun
Effect: On Enter: Banish a Sun you control. If you do, draw 2 cards and each opponent discards a card.
Lore: When Houyi shot down the ninth sun, a great shadow passed over the land. For a moment, the people forgot to grieve.

Name: Blazing Noon
Effect: On Enter: If there are 3+ Suns in play, banish all beings with 1 Power at any location. Ongoing: Your Suns here cannot be moved by opponent effects.
Lore: At noon, the ten suns clustered at the zenith. The sky turned white. Even the stone mountains cracked open with the heat.

Name: Storm Sun
Effect: On Enter: If there are 4+ Suns in play, banish all beings with 2 or less Power at this location. Ongoing: Your Suns at this location have +1 Power.
Lore: The great rivers retreated from their banks. The sea itself began to boil at the edges. Heaven watched, and said nothing.

Name: Jade Rabbit
Effect: On Enter: Draw a card. While on top: Your Deities at this location cannot be banished.
Lore: Chang'e, alone on the cold moon, keeps only one companion — the white rabbit who grinds the herb of immortality under the pale winter light.

Name: Sun Charioteer
Effect: On Enter: Move up to 2 Suns to any locations. While on top: Your Suns at this location have +1 Power.
Lore: Xihe's charioteers had steered the solar carriages for ten thousand years without fault. On the day the suns rebelled, they could only follow.

Name: Xihe, Mother of the Suns
Effect: On Enter: Return up to 2 Suns from your graveyard to your hand. While on top: At the start of your turn, draw a card if you control 3+ Suns.
Lore: Every morning she bathed her children in the sweet spring at the Fusang tree and sent them one by one across the sky. She never imagined they might disobey.

Name: Di Jun, Father of the Suns
Effect: On Enter: Put up to 3 Suns from your deck into play at any locations. While on top: At the start of your turn, if you control 5+ Suns, each opponent banishes one of their beings.
Lore: The Jade Emperor watched from his heavenly palace as his children set the world ablaze. He sent Houyi to frighten them back — not to kill them.

Name: Chang'e, the Moon Goddess
Effect: On Enter: Banish up to 2 Suns you control. For each Sun banished this way, draw a card and each opponent banishes one of their beings.
Lore: She stole the elixir of immortality from Houyi and drank it all. Weightless, she rose to the moon. She has not returned.

Name: Solar Drought
Effect: On Enter: Put this at a location you choose. While here: At the start of your turn, if there are 4+ Suns in play, each opponent must return a being from this location to their hand.
Lore: The crops failed in the first week. The lakes turned to salt flats in the second. By the third, people had stopped counting days and started counting the dead.

Name: Houyi, the Divine Archer
Effect: On Enter: Banish any number of Suns at any locations. Then banish all beings at all locations with Power equal to or less than the number of Suns banished this way.
Lore: He drew the bowstring nine times. Nine golden crows fell from the sky in flames. Di Jun, grief-stricken, stripped him of his immortality. The world was saved. Houyi was not.
